evidence: model.ExplorationResult imports exploration.Edge and exploration.RelationEdgeType: 'import pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.exploration.Edge; import pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.exploration.RelationEdgeType;'
model.ComparisonResult imports exploration.Edge and exploration.RelationEdgeType — same cross-package inward import as ExplorationResult
exploration.CollocateProfileComparator imports model.AdjectiveProfile, model.ComparisonResult, model.QueryResults — creating the return direction of the cycle
exploration.SemanticFieldExplorer imports model.CoreCollocate, model.DiscoveredNoun, model.ExplorationResult, model.PosGroup, model.QueryResults — confirming the bidirectional coupling
The net result is a genuine model ↔ exploration package cycle: two packages each importing types from the other
Edge and RelationEdgeType live in exploration/ even though they are consumed by model DTOs; moving them to model/ would break the cycle at zero cost
All other dependency directions are clean: api → {exploration, config, model, query}; query → model; config → {model, utils}; collections is a leaf
LongIntHashMap in collections/ is a pure leaf with no outward imports — correct placement post model-reorg

SemanticFieldExplorer hardcodes NOUN_CQL_CONSTRAINT = "[xpos=\"NN.*\"]"; CollocateProfileComparator hardcodes ADJECTIVE_PATTERN = "[xpos=\"JJ.*\"]"; both carry TODOs noting the grammar config should supply these — the cross-module coordination is deferred to future wiring

strengths: Package decomposition broadly mirrors domain capabilities: api, config, exploration, indexer, model, query, utils, collections, viz are all purposeful and non-overlapping
Dependency directions are correct in most directions: api depends on all subsystems; query, config, and exploration depend on model; collections is a true leaf
LongIntHashMap was properly relocated to a dedicated collections/ package rather than polluting utils/
Handler split (SketchHandlers, ExplorationHandlers) keeps the api/ package focused without creating a god class
GrammarConfig extraction into a value object separate from GrammarConfigLoader demonstrates correct loader/data separation
cross_module_architecture.issue_character: A single structural cycle — model ↔ exploration — where model DTOs import graph-edge types from the exploration package while exploration classes import model DTOs in return; this is a two-class cluster problem, not a diffuse systemic smell.
cross_module_architecture.score_rationale: The architecture is clearly intentional and mostly correct — packages are domain-aligned and dependency directions are clean in 7 of the 9 packages. However the bidirectional model ↔ exploration import creates a genuine package cycle: model.ExplorationResult and model.ComparisonResult import Edge and RelationEdgeType from the exploration package while the entire exploration package depends on model. This is a non-trivial structural defect because it couples the model package to a specific graph-edge representation that belongs in either the exploration or model package exclusively. The fix is straightforward (move Edge/RelationEdgeType to model/) but its absence keeps this from scoring as 'solid'.

issues.summary: Bidirectional package cycle: model.ExplorationResult/ComparisonResult import from exploration/ while all of exploration/ imports from model/
evidence: ComparisonResult.java: same two imports from exploration package
SemanticFieldExplorer imports model.ExplorationResult, model.CoreCollocate, model.DiscoveredNoun, model.PosGroup, model.QueryResults
model/ contains domain data; exploration/ contains algorithms — graph edge types belong to model/ or a shared graph-types package, not to exploration/
issues.suggestion: Move Edge.java and RelationEdgeType.java from exploration/ to model/. Update all imports. This breaks the cycle with minimal collateral change and places graph-edge value types where model-layer DTOs can import them without a circular dependency.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator hardcode English POS tag patterns instead of deriving them from RelationConfig, violating the config-driven boundary
evidence: SemanticFieldExplorer: 'private static final String NOUN_CQL_CONSTRAINT = "[xpos=\"NN.*\"]";' with TODO noting it should come from config
CollocateProfileComparator: 'private static final String ADJECTIVE_PATTERN = "[xpos=\"JJ.*\"]";' with same TODO

RelationConfig.collocateReversePattern() already provides the right pattern per PosGroup — but this method is not injected into CollocateProfileComparator or SemanticFieldExplorer's reverse-lookup path
issues.suggestion: Pass the appropriate RelationConfig (or its collocateReversePattern() result) into CollocateProfileComparator at construction time and into the phase-2 buildCollocateToNounsMap call in SemanticFieldExplorer. This removes the hardcoded English-only constraint and completes the config-driven boundary intent.

root_causes: Graph edge types (Edge, RelationEdgeType) were placed in exploration/ because they were first used there; the later addition of buildEdges() to model DTOs created the cycle without a corrective refactor
Config-injection for POS constraints was deferred with TODOs rather than completed during the exploration feature build
likely_symptoms: Any new model DTO that needs to produce graph edges must import exploration/, deepening the cycle over time
